property_history.description: Property sold by beneficiary for $715,000 within 2-year exemption period (1 year 8 months after death)

properties.notes:
**Batch 1,2,3 - Scenario 24: Deceased Estate - Two-Year Exemption**

Property was deceased's main residence. Beneficiary sold within 2 years of death, qualifying for full main residence exemption.

**Timeline:**
- 15 Jun 1995 to 10 Mar 2023: Main residence (deceased) - 27.73 years
- 10 Mar 2023: Death of owner - property passes to beneficiary
- 10 Mar 2023 to 15 Nov 2024: Deceased estate period (1.69 years - within 2-year limit)

**Key Rules:**
- Beneficiary's cost base: Market value at date of death ($685,000)
- Beneficiary sold within 2 years: Qualifies for full main residence exemption
- Capital gain for beneficiary: $715,000 - $685,000 - $20,075 = $9,925
- **NO CGT PAYABLE** - Two-year deceased estate exemption applies

**Expected Result:**
- NO CGT PAYABLE - Two-year deceased estate exemption (s118-195 ITAA 1997)

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- Section 118-195 ITAA 1997 (Deceased Estate Exemption)
- If beneficiary sells within 2 years of death, main residence exemption continues
- Section 128-15 ITAA 1997 (Market value cost base for beneficiary)
